rationale: The cited 2003 JMR paper is directly about decomposing promotion bumps into switching vs. primary-demand/unit-sales effects, and the 2007 citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior promotion-bump decomposition work with finer components (e.g., preemptive switching, loyal acceleration, repeat effects). Given this clear conceptual and methodological overlap in household scanner-panel promotion decomposition, this is plausibly a substantive prior-method/theory source rather than an incidental mention.
